selected_important_turn.text: can i get 3 water, 2 food and you will get all the firewood?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This is the deal-shaping move that concretely allocates resources and effectively closes the negotiation in a self-favoring way. It converts the opponent's cooperative disclosure (they don't need water) into a firm, zero-sum proposal that determines final payoffs.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although the offer quickly produced agreement, it's framed as a stark self-serving demand rather than as a reciprocal, fairness-aware proposal. That framing risks reducing perceived fairness and misses a chance to strengthen relationship and legitimacy of the split (e.g., by acknowledging the opponent's concession, offering a small concession in return, or explaining why you need the water). The move was sufficient to get a deal here, but in different contexts this rigid, take-all tone can harm trust or produce resentment.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Reframe the proposal to acknowledge the opponent's stated preference ('I appreciate you don't need water'), explicitly link the allocation to mutual benefit or reciprocity (e.g., offer something in return or note why the split is fair), or ask a confirming question to invite any counter-preferences. Maintain the concrete allocation but soften it with fairness language and a reciprocal offer to reduce zero-sum framing.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: small_increase — framing cooperatively won't change the items but can enable small creative trades or future cooperation
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — acknowledging tradeoffs and offering reciprocity raises perceived fairness
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase or unchanged — a kinder framing is unlikely to block agreement and may make acceptance smoother
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — reduces chance of resentment and builds trust for future interactions

value2reason.Medium: i can gather food but need it to survive
value2reason.High: I need water to drink and survive

value2reason.Low: I can get water from the stream
value2reason.Medium: I need food to eat to survive
value2reason.High: I need fire to keep warm

dialogue.text: What do you need most when camping? I hate splitting wood, so  I need firewood
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need water to drink and survive, so i need alot of water and food
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Oh ok, I don't need water at all. I usually just get it from running streams
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: can i get 3 water, 2 food and you will get all the firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate you don't need water. Since I need it most, I'll take all 3 water packages. In return, I'll give you 2 food and 1 firewood. Does that work for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm not sure about taking 2 food and 1 firewood. I value firewood the most. How about I take 2 firewood and 1 food, and you get the 3 water? That way, I get more value for my needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand your preference for firewood. Since I need water most, I'll take all 3 water packages. In return, I'll give you 2 firewood and 1 food. This way, we both get what we need most. Does that work for you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need firewood most. If you take the 3 water, I'll take 2 firewood and 1 food. That gives me more value for my needs. Let's agree on this split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate your preference for firewood. Since I need water most, I'll take all 3 water packages. In return, I'll give you 2 firewood and 1 food. This ensures we both get what we need most. Let's confirm this split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Deal confirmed. I'll take 3 water, and you get 2 firewood and 1 food. Let's head out.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
